rulings.Case Title: MANUELA ALVAREZ VS. WELLS FARGO BANK et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motion For An Order Dismissing Deft Wells Fargo From The Entire Action
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, August 16, line 1. DEFENDANT WELLS FARGO BANK SOUTHWEST, N.A., F/K/A WACHOVIA Ntc Of Motion For An Order Dismissing Deft Wells Fargo From The Entire Action OFF CALENDAR as moot. Dismissal filed July 24, 2013. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: CAITLIN FITTING et al VS. PAUL KRAAIJVANGER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Omtion For An Order Permitting Discovery Of Deft'S Financial Condition And Net Worth After Default Was Entered And Relief Therefrom Was Denied;
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, August 16, line 2. PLAINTIFF CAITLIN FITTING's Motion For An Order Permitting Discovery Of Defendant's Financial Condition And Net Worth After Default Was Entered And Relief Therefrom Was Denied DENIED. Moving party failed to sustain burden and failed to provide clear and convincing evidence of strong likelihood of prevailing on its claim pursuant to Section 3294 (fraud oppression or malice) by supporting affidavits. Moving party's reliance on entry of default is misplaced (Motion 4:22-23). Default admits only well pleaded facts alleged by plaintiff in the complaint, not contentions, deductions, or conclusions of fact or law. See Kim v. Westmoore Partners, Inc. (2011) 201 Cal.App.4th 267. Plaintiff's consolatory allegations that defendant's acts were "carried out in bad faith for the specific purpose of injuring Plaintiffs" (Complaint Paragraph 1); "for the purpose of harassing" (Complaint Paragraph 33); and "done intentionally and in bad faith" (Complaint Paragraph 42) is not a substitute for presenting "clear and convincing evidence" of "substantial probability" of prevailing by "appropriate affidavits." CCC 3294; CCP 3295(c). Since Plaintiff failed to sustain its initial burden, the Court does not reach the issue of propriety of opposition filed by defaulted defendant. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: PATRICK STACK VS. TYRA TAYLOR STRINGER et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, August 16, line 7. DEFENDANT TYRA STRINGER's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT Sustained in part and overruled in part. Defendant's general demurrer is overruled, complaint sufficiently alleges a cause of action against an unauthorized holdover tenant. Defendant's special demurrer is sustained with 5 day leave to amend to indicate the correct name of the tenant throughout the Complaint. = (501/REQ)
